ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Denii a hɔl bɛl u zuu a dia ú hum u huma, di a dia balii bɛl maa waa hoo.
translated: Denii holds the stomach where the food is, saying hum hum, and the child's stomach is sleeping.

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: A hɔl himmaanii Laban ligzi bubala la maa n lɛgzɛɛ gɛɛ di piizi, buniinazi la maa n chɛssɛɛ gɛɛ di lɛgzi ani pebibiŋŋe maa, di gil u biibaala ra binnaa.
translated: He called his sons; Laban took his sons and went to the field where the sheep were, and they ate and drank and rejoiced with him when the day was over.

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Ka ba ra pogzi di ra ŋɔ, “Kaali de! Anɛɛ hinnɛi? Yaa gillɔɔ i so yaa be, i jaa ra nyiŋŋi i ra bagli yaa gunla yaa n ninna ɛɛ! Yaa daŋ jaa tɔɔrii werge maa kaali gunla yaa n niŋ vii tɔɔri baala naa.” Denii ba jaa kiiri hel Lɔt dominua, di tau, ka ra nyiŋŋi bá chɔgi u gbera.
translated: When they arrived at the place, he said, “Look up, where is your brother? He told his companion, ‘When you see the land and the path, hurry and flee there, for I am bringing my sister-in-law out of the city.’ But his companion looked back and became a pillar of salt.”

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Bɔr kaa duani, hɔɔ kaa pizi. A hɔpizaa anue hinnɛ guŋ.
translated: Bor eats duani, Hoo eats pizi. He prepares it with a mortar and pestle.

ref: So do whatever God tells you to do. Even the property God took from our father and gave to you really belongs to us and our children.
text: Daama la maa Kɔraawiizi n lɛɛ yaa mɛɛ be di tɛi la, ɛɛ yaa ni yaa biizi horaa. Awia, ɛɛ gunla maa Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔ i be la.”
translated: Daama, our Kɔraawiizi is not here, he has gone to take care of the children. Well, let's give our Kɔraawiizi a hand when he comes back.
